_meta.keyKnowledge: We are learning that writers create sequence and cohesion by deliberately repeating text structures, language features and vocabulary throughout a text.

_source.w: We are learning that writers create sequence and cohesion by deliberately repeating text structures, language features and vocabulary throughout a text.
y: Intentional repetition creates rhythm, emphasis and coherence in writing.
Understanding how cohesion works helps us create texts that flow smoothly and hold together.
This technique is used by skilled writers in every genre — from poetry to academic essays.
eg: We identify how an author repeats a phrase or structure throughout a text for emphasis or unity.
We analyse how vocabulary chains — groups of related words — create cohesion in a paragraph.
We discuss how repeated text structures (like recurring verse patterns in poems) create rhythm and predictability.
We experiment with deliberate repetition in our own writing to create effect.
We compare a cohesive text with a disjointed one and identify what makes the difference.
We use referencing, synonyms and word chains to connect ideas across paragraphs in our own writing.
